target_game.name: Flip & Dive 3D
target_game.core_loop: Jump from a high platform, control the fall to perform tricks, and aim for a perfect entry into the water pool to maximize depth.
target_game.audience_proxy: Broad casual audience, teens and young adults, looking for quick, satisfying physics-based gameplay.
target_game.visual_style: Minimalist 3D with bright, contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes.

palette.description: Vibrant contrast between light blue environments and a bright pink character, with bold purple and blue UI banners.

top_archetypes.label: Fail · in-game

game_fit_scores.rationale: This is a licensed anime IP static ad (Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle) — completely off-brand for Flip & Dive 3D, which is a minimalist physics hypercasual with no anime, combat, or energy-blast vocabulary. Mechanic fit is near zero: there's no jumping/diving/timing read, just a charged-pose CTA, and we obviously can't borrow Goku's likeness. Only marginal audience overlap via broad young-male casual gamers, but the IP-dependent hook is non-replicable and would mislead installers — do not pursue.

This is a Dragon Ball Z anime IP hook (Goku SSJ3 Dragon Fist) with gold/indigo/purple combat palette — it has zero overlap with Flip & Dive 3D's minimalist pastel-blue pool diving aesthetic, ragdoll humor tone, or timing/aiming core loop. Repurposing licensed anime IP also carries serious legal/ASA risk and would mislead users into installing a physics arcade, tanking IPM and retention. Hard pass; do not adapt unless reframed as a generic "power-up dive" trick with original art, which would no longer resemble this archetype.

The "relaxing puzzle" hook with a hand-on-stylus bus-sorting demo fundamentally mismatches Flip & Dive's core loop, which is about kinetic ragdoll thrills, timing and aerial tricks — not top-down logic puzzles. Visual frictions are significant: the archetype's orange/blue/purple puzzle aesthetic lacks the bright pink character and pool/height-marker signals that make Flip & Dive scroll-stoppable. Audience overlap exists at the broad casual/relaxation level, but adapting this template would require replacing the bus puzzle with a finger-guided dive trajectory plus VO ("ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?") — only worth testing as a low-priority hook variant, not a primary archetype.

Hard pass for Flip & Dive 3D. The dark earthy palette (navy/orange/forest green) and architectural cross-section animation clash directly with the game's bright pastel-blue + neon pink minimalist 3D style, and there's no humanoid ragdoll, pool, or depth counter to anchor recognition. The only thin thread is a "downward reveal" motif that loosely echoes diving depth — but retrofitting bunker-curiosity hooks onto a physics flip game would feel like a bait-and-switch and likely tank IPM. Skip this archetype; if curiosity is the desired pitch, commission a "how deep can you go?" depth-reveal variant in the game's native palette instead.

Hard pass on adapting this archetype literally — the bus-crash/parking-puzzle hook is mechanically alien to Flip & Dive's jump-aim-splash loop, and the white/red/green palette clashes with the game's signature pink-on-cyan identity. The only transferable element is the broader "fail bait + WHY IS THIS SO HARD" framing, which does map to hypercasual audiences; if we test it, abstract just the text-overlay/fail-loop structure (e.g., diver belly-flops onto concrete, missing the pool) rather than the bus scenario itself. Low confidence cluster anyway (1 creative, 259d old, single advertiser) — not worth prioritizing over fresher fail-bait references.

brief.title: "Ever Played Something This Relaxing?" — Finger-Guided Dive
brief.hook_3s: A finger drags a glowing trajectory arc from a sky-high platform down into a tiny pink pool — VO whispers: "Ever played a game that actually feels relaxing?"
scene_flow: 0–3s: Top-down-ish angled view of the dive platform, a fingertip enters frame and slowly traces a curved dotted aim line toward the pool below while VO asks the relaxation question.
3–7s: Finger releases — the bright pink ragdoll character tips off the platform in slow-mo, height markers ticking down (50m, 30m, 10m) with soft chimes per trick rotation.
7–11s: Camera follows the spin; finger taps to time a perfect tuck — a subtle purple ring pulses on each successful flip, ASMR-style 'thunk' on each rotation.
11–14s: Near-vertical entry, splash collapses inward (low-splash = perfect), depth counter rockets past -42m on a bold magenta banner.
14–15s: Pool ripples calm to a flat mirror, end card: pink character silhouette + CTA.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Flip & Dive 3D's signature minimalist 3D look: pale #AEE2FF sky and water, geometric white platform stack, the hot #FF007F ragdoll as the only saturated character element, and #C754D9 UI banners for depth/height callouts. Add an overlaid translucent fingertip + dotted aim arc to mimic the "hand solving a puzzle" archetype, but here the puzzle IS the dive trajectory. Soft cinematic slow-mo, gentle bloom, no clutter — the calm/satisfying mood is sold by negative space and crisp geometric shapes.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Flip & Dive rotation is all silent gameplay videos and one playable — zero VO-driven "relaxation question" hooks, which is the trending satisfaction-mixed pattern from the market scan. This brief grafts that proven UGC-style narrated opener onto authentic Flip & Dive mechanics (finger-traced dive arc instead of bus-sorting), giving UA a fresh top-of-funnel angle to A/B against the existing mute creatives without cannibalizing them. Test as a low-budget variant; if CTR lifts, scale.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes: a tall white tiered diving platform on the left rising into a pale #AEE2FF sky, a small turquoise swimming pool far below on flat ground, a bright #FF007F humanoid ragdoll character mid-crouch at the platform edge ready to leap, a translucent fingertip overlay tracing a dotted curved aim line from character down into the pool, height markers '50m / 30m / 10m' along the side in clean sans-serif, a bold #C754D9 banner across the upper third reading 'Ever played something THIS relaxing?', soft bloom, calm satisfying mood, authentic Flip & Dive 3D in-game HUD chrome, no extra characters, no stock UI. [Audio: VO (soft female, ASMR-close mic): 'Ever played a game that actually feels relaxing?' / chillhop lo-fi 70 BPM, mellow Rhodes piano / soft fingertip-tap on screen at 0.5s, gentle wind whoosh at 2s.]

Storyboard frame mid-dive, aspect 9:16, minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes: the #FF007F ragdoll character tucked in a flip mid-air against a #AEE2FF sky gradient, motion-blur arc trailing behind in faint #C754D9, the small turquoise pool visible directly below in the lower third, a depth/height marker column on the right showing '20m', a clean #C754D9 banner overlay reading 'Time the flips ✦', authentic Flip & Dive 3D HUD with simple geometric platform stub at top of frame, no extra characters, soft shadows, satisfying slow-mo feel. [Audio: silent VO / calm satisfying lo-fi 70 BPM continues / soft 'whoomp' whoosh per rotation, subtle chime on flip-complete at 2.5s.]

Storyboard end-card frame, aspect 9:16, minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes: top-down view of a small turquoise rectangular pool centered on flat #AEE2FF ground, a tiny pinpoint #FF007F splash ripple at the exact center showing a perfect entry, a huge bold #C754D9 banner across the middle reading 'PERFECT ENTRY  -42m', clean depth counter UI at the bottom, authentic Flip & Dive 3D minimalist HUD, no character visible above water, calm mirror-like ripples, satisfying negative space. [Audio: VO (soft, smiling): 'Yeah… that kind of relaxing.' / lo-fi resolves on warm major chord / clean low-splash 'plip' SFX at 0.3s, soft success chime at 1.5s.]

brief.title: How Deep Can You Go? — Cross-Section Curiosity Dive
brief.hook_3s: A pastel-blue pool cross-section peels open mid-splash — revealing an impossibly deep shaft plunging past height markers labeled 50m… 200m… ???
scene_flow: 0-3s: Pink ragdoll launches off the high platform; camera tracks the flip in classic Flip & Dive framing against #AEE2FF sky.
3-7s: Splash hits — camera doesn't cut. The water surface peels away cross-section style, exposing a vertical shaft below the pool with depth markers ticking past.
7-11s: Ragdoll keeps sinking past secret layers (neon pink coral, purple geometric caverns, glowing #C754D9 abyss) as the depth counter spins wildly.
11-14s: Counter freezes at a comically huge number with a '???' — bold pink banner slams in: HOW DEEP CAN YOU GO?
14-15s: Tap-to-play finger taps the jump button, loop teases next dive.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Flip & Dive 3D's minimalist 3D look: flat-shaded geometric platforms, soft #AEE2FF sky-and-water gradient, the signature hot-pink (#FF007F) humanoid ragdoll, and #C754D9 purple banners for HUD. The twist is a never-before-seen cross-section camera move — the pool's side wall slices open like a diagram so we see the dive continue underground through stacked, color-blocked depth zones. Keep lighting soft and satisfying, no shadows harsher than the base game; depth counter and height markers stay in the game's native bold sans-serif chrome.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current rotation on Flip & Dive 3D is all unbranded gameplay videos and a generic playable — zero curiosity-driven hooks despite curiosity being a proven trending pitch. This brief grafts the archetype's "cross-section reveal" structure onto the game's native depth-mechanic, swapping bunkers for a literal pool-floor reveal so it stays IPM-safe and on-palette. UA team: run as a 15s video first to validate the depth-reveal hook, then port the winning frame into a playable end-card.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, Minimalist 3D with bright, contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes. A bright #FF007F humanoid ragdoll mid-flip diving headfirst into a small circular #AEE2FF swimming pool, but the pool's side wall is sliced open like an architectural cross-section, revealing a vertical shaft plunging deep below with stacked depth markers (-50m, -200m) and a glowing #C754D9 purple abyss at the bottom. Bold pink banner overlay reads 'HOW DEEP CAN YOU GO?' in the game's chunky sans-serif. Soft pastel lighting, flat shading, in-game HUD with depth counter top-right, height markers along the platform edge. Authentic Flip & Dive 3D in-game moment, no realism, no shadows. [Audio: VO (curious whisper) 'Wait… how deep does it actually go?' / calm satisfying lo-fi at 80 BPM with soft synth pads / whoosh on flip at 0.5s, muted splash at 2s, ascending chime as depth counter ticks past 200m]

Storyboard frame 1, aspect 9:16, Minimalist 3D with bright, contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes. Side-view of the tall #AEE2FF blue diving platform with height markers (10m, 20m, 30m) running up its edge in #C754D9 purple, a tiny #FF007F pink ragdoll character crouched at the top about to jump, small circular pool far below. Game's native UI: depth counter '0m' top-right in a purple banner, tap indicator pulsing on jump button. Overlay text 'Perfect entry…' in bold white sans-serif. Flat-shaded, soft pastel sky gradient, authentic Flip & Dive 3D framing. [Audio: VO 'Looks like a normal dive…' / calm satisfying ambient pad at 80 BPM / soft wind whoosh, single UI tick on tap indicator]

Storyboard frame 2, aspect 9:16, Minimalist 3D with bright, contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes. Vertical cross-section view: top third shows the #AEE2FF pool surface with splash ripples, bottom two-thirds reveal a hidden underwater shaft descending past geometric layers — pink coral-block formations, purple #C754D9 crystal caverns, a deep glowing void. The #FF007F ragdoll is shown as a small silhouette sinking through the layers. Depth counter HUD spinning '-347m' in a purple banner. Bold overlay text '-200m' flashing in pink. Game's native flat-shaded style, no realism. [Audio: VO (wide-eyed) 'No way…' / calm satisfying lo-fi swelling at 85 BPM / bubbling underwater SFX, rapid tick-tick-tick on depth counter, low purple synth drone as layers reveal]

brief.title: Why Is This Dive So Hard?
brief.hook_3s: Pink ragdoll launches off a towering cyan platform — and pancakes onto concrete two feet from the pool.
scene_flow: 0–3s: POV looks down from a tall cyan diving tower. A finger taps JUMP — the bright pink humanoid leaps. Overlay: 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?'
3–7s: Mid-air flip with depth/height markers ticking. Player mistimes the straighten — ragdoll over-rotates. Overlay: 'JUST AIM FOR THE POOL…'
7–10s: Belly-flop SPLAT onto the pink tile deck, missing the tiny blue pool by inches. Depth counter reads 0.0m. Overlay: 'BRO 💀'
10–13s: Quick retry montage — 3 more cringe fails (head-first into ladder, bounces off umbrella, lands in pink banner). Overlay: '99% CAN'T DO IT'
13–15s: Final attempt teases a clean entry, freezes mid-flip. Overlay: 'CAN YOU?' → CTA card.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Flip & Dive 3D's signature look: pastel cyan (#AEE2FF) sky and tiled deck, hot-pink (#FF007F) ragdoll character, purple (#C754D9) UI banners and depth markers. Minimalist 3D geometry — chunky platforms, no textures, soft ambient occlusion. Camera mimics the in-game side/orbit angle with the same depth-meter HUD, JUMP button, and bold banner ribbons. Fails are exaggerated with squash-and-stretch ragdoll physics; impact frames freeze for a half-beat with a purple shockwave ring. Keep it clean and toy-like — no gritty crash imagery.
text_overlays: WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Flip & Dive rotation is all silent gameplay loops with zero copy or emotional framing — they have no fail-bait creative in market. This brief imports the trending "WHY IS THIS SO HARD" fail-loop structure (abstracted from the bus archetype, which is mechanically irrelevant) and grafts it onto the game's native belly-flop comedy, giving UA a challenge-driven hook to A/B against the existing wordless videos. Test as a 15s video first; if CPI beats the silent baseline, port the cringe-fail montage into the playable.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical aspect ratio, authentic in-game moment from Flip & Dive 3D: bright hot-pink (#FF007F) humanoid ragdoll mid-belly-flop, frozen one inch above a pastel cyan (#AEE2FF) tiled pool deck, tiny square swimming pool just out of reach, tall minimalist 3D diving tower in background, purple (#C754D9) depth-counter HUD reading 0.0m in top corner, bold purple banner across the middle reading 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?', minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes, no textures, soft shadows, toy-like proportions, same UI chrome as Flip & Dive 3D screenshots. [Audio: VO (frustrated male, gen-z): 'bro why is this so hard' / lo-fi calm-satisfying beat 90 BPM with playful marimba / wet SPLAT thud at 1.5s, soft whoosh on fall]

Storyboard frame, 9:16 vertical, authentic Flip & Dive 3D gameplay shot from low side angle: pink ragdoll character over-rotating in mid-air above a small cyan (#AEE2FF) square pool, purple (#C754D9) height markers ticking down the tower edge, JUMP button faded at bottom, bold pink (#FF007F) overlay banner reading 'JUST AIM FOR THE POOL…', minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes, clean pastel sky, no textures. [Audio: VO: 'easy right?' / same calm marimba beat continuing / airy whoosh on flip, ticking timing-meter SFX]

Storyboard frame, 9:16 vertical, authentic Flip & Dive 3D fail moment: pink (#FF007F) ragdoll splattered flat on cyan (#AEE2FF) tile deck two feet from the tiny pool, limbs bent comically, purple (#C754D9) shockwave ring radiating from impact, depth counter showing 0.0m, bold purple banner reading '99% CAN'T DO IT', minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple geometric shapes, same HUD as Flip & Dive 3D. [Audio: VO: 'nope' / beat drops to half-time / cartoon SPLAT plus low fail-buzzer at 0.8s, crowd 'oof' sting]
